01/22/2025 James Stothard: Internal meeting with our legal team to discuss the customers' requests 

Meeting Recap – Barclays Contenly Renewal Legal Discussion

The team reviewed Barclays’ legal redlines for the Contently renewal, noting that while the customer’s requirements are extensive, most are not considered deal-breakers. The approach will be to accept reasonable terms and push back only on critical issues, mirroring the strategy used with Carlisle.

Barclays does not actively use the Contently platform (no logins in 4–5 years); their main interaction is receiving monthly emails with financial transactions and screenshots. This minimizes data exposure, but the team agreed to delete unused accounts to further reduce risk.

Key legal discussion points included:
Notification obligations if services are disrupted, even though only professional services (not SaaS) are provided.
Ensuring AWS is listed as a material subcontractor, but limiting disclosure of other affiliates to Trilogy, Crossover, and AWS.
The need for a disaster recovery/business continuity plan (confirmed as available and tested annually).
Acceptance and review of deliverables: Barclays reviews and approves content, which is standard procedure.
No software code is produced for Barclays; services are limited to content and design.
Background checks: All Trilogy/Crossover employees undergo checks; freelancers may need checks if accessing Barclays systems.
Environmental, anti-slavery, and other compliance clauses were discussed; the team is comfortable accepting standard language.

Commercial terms:
The renewal is for $66,875 (25% increase), considered low for the level of legal negotiation required.
Barclays is requesting a liability cap of £2M; the team will propose a lower cap (e.g., 5x contract value) and seek management approval.
Indemnity and limitation of liability are the most contentious issues; the team will attempt to cap IP infringement exposure and clarify that Barclays owns all deliverables.
Termination for convenience is a no-go, but customers can terminate and retain their content bank balance.

Next steps:
Legal will redline the agreement and circulate for internal review by Friday.
Uba has a follow-up call with Barclays to discuss commercial points.
James to update the ticket with the latest quote and special terms.
